sections.paragraph:
(either of a “stone-quarry,” from the root תְּבַר i.q. שָׁבַר, or a “lofty place,” umbilicus, i.q. טַבּוּר which see), [Tabor], pr.n.-

(1) of a mountain on the borders of Zebulon and Naphtali, situated in the middle of a plain, called by Josephus Ἰταβύριον, Ἀταβύριον (Relandi Palæstina, p. 331-336), now جبل طور (Burckhardt’s Reisen, p. 589, seq.), Jos 19:22 Jud. 4:6 8:18 Psa 89:13 Jer 46:18 Hos 5:1.-(2) of an oak in the tribe of Benjamin, 1Sa 10:3.-(3) of a town of the Levites in the tribe of Zebulon, 1Ch 6:62.
